summary: Hardened the CodeGraph helper scripts so they default to loopback-only SurrealDB access, require explicit CODEGRAPH_SURREAL_USER and CODEGRAPH_SURREAL_PASS credentials, and no longer embed or document root/root plus 0.0.0.0 defaults. Updated the active security plan to record the completed first milestone.
known_risks: There is no existing shell-test owner seam for these helper scripts, so proof stays scoped to forbidden-pattern checks, bash parsing, and the touched-file pre-commit hooks.
required_tests: bash -n scripts/codegraph-start-db.sh && bash -n scripts/codegraph-import.sh && bash -n scripts/codegraph-index-export.sh

commands: bash -n scripts/codegraph-start-db.sh && bash -n scripts/codegraph-import.sh && bash -n scripts/codegraph-index-export.sh
notes: The helper scripts now document the exact CODEGRAPH_SURREAL_* environment variables they expect and default their bind and connection URLs to loopback-only endpoints.
Plan 61 remains active because the publish workflow, npm monitoring, and repo-audit enforcement milestones are still open even though the first helper-script milestone is complete.

reviewer_notes: The checked-in scripts no longer advertise or execute 0.0.0.0:3004 with root/root defaults.
The plan now records the first completed security milestone without widening into publish or repo-audit enforcement work.
